tr.td: 1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily
1 tablet (10 mg) by mouth once daily
Albuterol (as albuterol sulfate) 830 microgram/mL solution for inhalation
2.5 mg (3 mL of 0.083%) via nebulizer every 4 to 6 hours as needed
2 sprays (50 micrograms per spray) in each nostril once daily
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth every 4 to 6 hours as needed
Influenza virus antigen only vaccine product
Clostridium tetani toxoid antigen adsorbed only vaccine product
Live attenuated Human alphaherpesvirus 3 only vaccine product
Assessment of health and social care needs
Depression screening using Patient Health Questionnaire Two-Item score (procedure)
Excision of fallopian tube and surgical removal of ectopic pregnancy
Inadequate digital access affecting health care

ul.li: Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve a 5% reduction in body weight to improve blood pressure control.
Limit daily sodium intake to less than 2.3 grams to support blood pressure control.
Engage in at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity.
Patient will participate in diabetes self-management education and demonstrate understanding of blood glucose monitoring, healthy eating, and physical activity recommendations.
Patient will achieve and maintain HbA1c below 5.7% to reduce risk of progression to diabetes.
Patient will achieve at least 5% weight loss from baseline to improve glycemic control and reduce risk of diabetes.
Patient will engage in at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity physical activity.
Patient will demonstrate effective self-management of asthma symptoms and triggers.

tr.td: Pain severity - 0-10 verbal numeric rating [Score] - Reported
Cholesterol in LDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma by Direct assay
Cholesterol in HDL [Mass/volume] in Serum or Plasma
1 tablet (25 mg) by mouth once daily

ul.li: Maintain blood pressure below 130/80 mmHg through lifestyle modifications.
Achieve a 5% reduction in body weight to improve blood pressure control.
Limit daily sodium intake to less than 2.3 grams to support blood pressure control.
Engage in at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity aerobic physical activity.
Patient will participate in diabetes self-management education and demonstrate understanding of blood glucose monitoring, healthy eating, and physical activity recommendations.
Patient will achieve and maintain HbA1c below 5.7% to reduce risk of progression to diabetes.
Patient will achieve at least 5% weight loss from baseline to improve glycemic control and reduce risk of diabetes.
Patient will engage in at least 150 minutes per week of moderate-intensity physical activity.
Patient will demonstrate effective self-management of asthma symptoms and triggers.
